records.ety: [F. cantine bottle case, canteen (cf. Sp. & It. cantina cellar, bottle case), either contr. fr. It. canovettina, dim. of canova cellar, or, more likely, fr. OF. cant. corner, It. & Sp. canto. See 1st Cant.]

defs: A small vessel used by soldiers or hikers for carrying water, liquor, or other drink.
notes: ☞ In 1910 in the English service the canteen is made of wood and holds three pints; in the United States it is usually a tin flask.

defs: A chest containing culinary and other vessels for military officers in a garrison.
The sutler's shop in a garrison.

A store or small shop within a larger establishment where refreshments and sometimes other supplies are sold. At a military base the canteen may be as large as a general store; within a school or small company it may be only a small counter with very limited supplies, or a snack bar.
A temporary location where food is dispensed during an emergency.
